# rust-bufrw0.2

## Publication status

The package is independently eligible and enabled for configured SCM COPR
publication. The canonical crates.io archive is pinned by SHA-256, fetched
directly from the immutable static registry endpoint, and includes the declared
MIT license file.

No exact `bufrw 0.2.0` provider exists in Fedora 43, Fedora 44, Rawhide, or
matching RPM Fusion repositories. The normalized crate manifest has no runtime
dependencies. The retained Fedora-specific patch removes only benchmark and
Criterion metadata, so it does not alter library behavior. This package must
publish before `rust-dbase0.8`.
